message:
chore: Sync beads after closing bd-1jt.1.2, bd-1jt.2.8, bd-1jt.7.2

Closed beads:
- bd-1jt.1.2: Watch command implementation complete
- bd-1jt.2.8: Host health checking verified and closed
- bd-1jt.7.2: Doctor preflight checks verified and closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.10","title":"Implement release verification + retry/resume uploads","description":"# Implement Release Verification + Retry/Resume Uploads

## Purpose
Ensure GitHub releases contain the exact expected assets, and recover safely from partial upload failures.

## Verification Steps
- Compare release assets against build manifest
- Verify checksums.sha256 matches uploaded binaries
- Optionally re-download and verify sha256 for spot checks

## Retry Strategy
- Use `gh release upload --clobber` for failed assets
- Track failed uploads in state file for resume
- Idempotent uploads (safe to re-run)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing assets detected and reported
- [ ] Partial uploads can be resumed safely
- [ ] Release verification produces JSON report
- [ ] Re-running verification does not create duplicate assets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:55:33.160035551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.415821448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.651659081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:40.580783022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.11","title":"Add pre-release quality gates (typecheck/lint/test) per tool","description":"# Add Pre-Release Quality Gates (typecheck/lint/test) per Tool

## Purpose
Ensure every release passes the tool's own quality checks before upload to GitHub.

## Behavior
- Read `checks` from `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Run checks in deterministic order
- Support `--skip-checks` and `--dry-run`
- Log per-check duration + exit code
- Fail release if any check fails (exit 1/6 depending on stage)

## Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout (JSON): list of checks, durations, exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checks run per-tool before release upload
- [ ] Failures block release with clear errors
- [ ] JSON output includes per-check results
- [ ] Dry-run shows planned checks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:25.414490480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:21.256627491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:47.402939311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes
- [ ] Dry-run available and deterministic
- [ ] Validation commands executed when available
- [ ] Works for all tools without touching `/data/projects`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629580772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:56.108363071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629546588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.3.3","title":"Implement minisign artifact signing","description":"# Implement Minisign Artifact Signing

## Purpose
Sign every release artifact so users can verify authenticity.

## Requirements
- Sign every binary + checksums.sha256
- Store signatures as `.minisig` files
- Use password-protected key from secrets store

## Installer Integration
- Verify signatures when minisign present
- Fail when `--require-signatures` is set

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All binaries + checksum file signed
- [ ] Signatures uploaded with releases
- [ ] Installers verify signatures","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:59.730105373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:13.612167551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:59.730105373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:24:03.620154033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.4","title":"Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for releases","description":"# Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for Releases

## Purpose
Produce SBOMs for each release to improve supply-chain transparency.

## Formats
- SPDX JSON
- CycloneDX JSON

## Behavior
- Generate SBOMs after build, before release
- Attach as release assets
- For container images, attach SBOM attestation if enabled

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SBOMs generated for each release
- [ ] Uploaded to GitHub release
- [ ] Works for Rust/Go/Bun projects","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:40.102566527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:47.963689719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.5","title":"Implement checksum auto-sync across flywheel repos","description":"# Checksum Auto-Sync Across Flywheel Repos

## Purpose
Update checksum manifests in downstream repos when installers change.

## Behavior
- Triggered after dsr release
- Recalculate checksums, update target repo in /tmp (never `/data/projects`)
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations
- For external tools, open a security review issue instead of auto-merge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksums recalculated after each release
- [ ] External changes flagged for review
- [ ] JSON output for verification","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:53:30.421759326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.145176916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.6","title":"Implement SLSA provenance attestation for supply chain security","description":"# Implement SLSA Provenance Attestation

## Purpose
Generate SLSA v1 provenance (in-toto Statement + predicate) for release artifacts.

## Required Fields
- `_type`: in-toto Statement v1
- `predicateType`: https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1
- `subject[]`: artifact name + sha256 digest
- `predicate.buildDefinition`: buildType + externalParameters
- `predicate.runDetails`: builder id + timestamps + invocation id

## Behavior
- Generate provenance for each release (one per tool/version)
- Attach as release asset
- For containers, attach cosign attestation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provenance generated for each artifact set
- [ ] Attached to GitHub release
- [ ] Verifiable with standard SLSA tooling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:38.736005543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:38.288053284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.805508188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:38.736005543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -41,7 +41,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.9","title":"Implement minisign key management and secure storage","description":"# Minisign Key Management and Secure Storage

## Purpose
Create and protect the Minisign keypair used to sign release artifacts.

## Key Generation (Minisign)
```bash
minisign -G -p minisign.pub -s ~/.config/dsr/secrets/minisign.key
```
- Private key must be **password-protected**
- Public key is embedded in installers and docs

## Storage Policy
- Private key: `~/.config/dsr/secrets/minisign.key` (chmod 600)
- Public key: `~/.config/dsr/minisign.pub` + published in README
- Never store unencrypted key in repo or CI logs

## Distribution Strategy
Recommended: **central signing** on trj after artifact collection. Avoid copying private keys to multiple hosts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Keypair generated with password prompt
- [ ] Public key documented and embedded
- [ ] Private key stored with strict permissions
- [ ] Signing workflow uses central host only

## References
- https://jedisct1.github.io/minisign/","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:38.864145251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:09:48.606073123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:09:48.606007460Z","close_reason":"Implemented minisign key management in src/signing.sh with functions for key generation (signing_init), verification (signing_check), artifact signing (signing_sign), and batch operations (signing_sign_batch). Added signing subcommand to dsr CLI. Keys stored in ~/.config/dsr/secrets/ with chmod 600 permissions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:38.864145251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4","title":"Theme 4: Smart Curl-Bash Installers","description":"# Smart Curl-Bash Installers

## Purpose
Create intelligent install scripts for each tool that:
1. Try to download latest release binary first (fastest)
2. Detect if release is stale (>10 commits behind HEAD)
3. Offer to build from source if stale or binary unavailable
4. Auto-install required toolchains if user consents

## Installer URL Pattern
Each tool gets a hosted installer:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/{tool}/main/install.sh
- Or via short URL: curl -sSL https://install.{tool}.dev | bash

## Usage
```bash
# Quick install (tries binary first)
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash

# Force source build
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --source

# Install specific version
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --version v1.5.0

# Install to custom location
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix ~/.local

# Non-interactive (CI mode)
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --yes
```

## Installer Logic Flow
```
1. Detect OS and Architecture
   └─→ Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64, macOS ARM64, macOS x86_64, Windows

2. Check Latest Release
   └─→ gh api repos/Dicklesworthstone/{tool}/releases/latest (or curl fallback)

3. Compare Release Freshness
   └─→ Count commits between release tag and HEAD
   └─→ If >10 commits behind, release is "stale"

4. Decision Point
   ├─→ Release exists AND fresh (<10 commits behind)?
   │   └─→ Download binary → Install → Done
   │
   └─→ Release missing OR stale?
       └─→ Prompt: "Build from source? [y/N]"
           ├─→ No  → Install stale release anyway (with warning)
           └─→ Yes → Check toolchain → Build → Install
```

## Freshness Check Implementation (Best Practices)
- Prefer GitHub compare API via `gh_api` wrapper (JSON only)
- If repo already exists locally, use git plumbing: `git rev-list --left-right --count <tag>...<branch>`
- Never parse human output from `git status` or `gh run list`

## Toolchain Auto-Installation
When building from source, check and offer to install missing toolchains:

### Rust
```bash
if ! command -v cargo &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Rust toolchain not found."
  echo "Install Rust nightly? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y --default-toolchain nightly
    source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
  else
    die "Rust required for source build"
  fi
fi
```

### Go
```bash
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Go toolchain not found."
  echo "Install latest Go? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    # Detect OS/arch and download appropriate binary
    GO_VERSION="1.23.0"
    GO_OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    GO_ARCH=$(uname -m | sed 's/x86_64/amd64/;s/aarch64/arm64/')
    curl -sSL "https://go.dev/dl/go${GO_VERSION}.${GO_OS}-${GO_ARCH}.tar.gz" | sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
    export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
  fi
fi
```

### Bun
```bash
if ! command -v bun &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Bun not found."
  echo "Install Bun? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
    source "$HOME/.bun/bin/bun"
  fi
fi
```

## Cross-Platform Considerations

### macOS
- Use curl (built-in) not wget
- Install to ~/.local/bin (user-writable) by default
- Handle Gatekeeper for unsigned binaries (xattr -d com.apple.quarantine)

### Linux
- Check for sudo if installing to /usr/local/bin
- Support both apt and rpm-based distros for dependencies
- Handle SELinux contexts if necessary

### Windows (WSL or Git Bash)
- Detect WSL vs native Windows
- Use .exe extension for binaries
- Handle PATH differences

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Linux x86_64, ARM64
- [ ] Works on macOS ARM64, x86_64
- [ ] Works on Windows (WSL at minimum)
- [ ] Correctly detects stale releases
- [ ] Toolchain installation is non-destructive
- [ ] --yes flag for CI/automation
- [ ] Detailed logging with --verbose
- [ ] Graceful failure with clear error messages

## Security Considerations
- Verify checksums after download
- Use HTTPS exclusively
- Don't execute arbitrary code from failed downloads
- Warn users about piping to bash","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:54.174177755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:35:43.089236552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:54.174177755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.1","title":"Implement install.sh template generator","description":"# Implement install.sh Template Generator

## Purpose
Generate per-tool install scripts from a shared template while enforcing consistent safety, logging, and verification rules.

## Template Requirements
- Shebang: `#!/usr/bin/env bash`
- `set -uo pipefail` (no `set -e`)
- Explicit error handling for download/build/verify steps
- stderr for human logs, stdout for JSON/paths if `--json`

## Inputs (from repos.d)
- `repos.d/<tool>.yaml` supplies: tool name, repo, language, build_cmd, binary_name
- targets, artifact naming, archive format
- pre-release checks (if used by installer)

## Output
Generated scripts to `./installers/{tool}/install.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Placeholders substituted correctly
- [ ] Generated scripts pass ShellCheck (warning+)
- [ ] Binary download + checksum + optional minisign verification works
- [ ] Cache/offline mode supported (if enabled)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:20.508569888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:33.386577729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.476070991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.919659290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:20.508569888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:14.062990057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:54.094130972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:45.431689318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.4.2","title":"Implement toolchain version detection and auto-installation in installers","description":"# Toolchain Version Detection and Auto-Installation

## Purpose
Detect missing/outdated toolchains and offer to install them **without** breaking existing setups.

## Rules
- Never overwrite existing toolchains without explicit user consent
- Never auto-delete existing installs (print instructions instead)
- Respect `--non-interactive` (fail with actionable message)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects existing installations correctly
- [ ] Version checks work on all platforms
- [ ] Installation does not break existing setups
- [ ] Clear manual upgrade instructions provided","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:27.090946781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1jt.4.2","title":"Implement toolchain version detection and auto-installation in installers","description":"# Toolchain Version Detection and Auto-Installation

## Purpose
Detect missing/outdated toolchains and offer to install them **without** breaking existing setups.

## Rules
- Never overwrite existing toolchains without explicit user consent
- Never auto-delete existing installs (print instructions instead)
- Respect `--non-interactive` (fail with actionable message)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects existing installations correctly
- [ ] Version checks work on all platforms
- [ ] Installation does not break existing setups

 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.3","title":"Add minisign verification to install.sh (optional but recommended)","description":"# Add Minisign Verification to install.sh (Optional but Recommended)

## Purpose
Verify downloaded binaries with minisign signatures to prevent tampering.

## Behavior
- If minisign available: verify by default
- If missing: warn, optionally install, or fail when --require-signatures
- Embed public key in installer (from dsr config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.sh verifies .minisig when available
- [ ] Clear failure if signature invalid
- [ ] Non-interactive mode supports --require-signatures","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:24.702985217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.206972391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.459042194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.697458578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.4","title":"Add installer cache/offline mode + gh release download fallback","description":"# Add Installer Cache/Offline Mode + gh release download Fallback

## Purpose
Improve reliability when network is flaky by caching release assets and allowing offline installs.

## Behavior
- Cache downloads in ~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>
- Support --cache-dir and --offline flags
- Prefer gh release download when gh is available (auth + retries)
- Fallback to curl when gh is missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cached assets reused when available
- [ ] Offline mode fails fast with clear error if cache missing
- [ ] gh release download works for private repos","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:35.225335336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.979791158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:04.030679358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

## Purpose
Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

## Behavior
- Detect OS/arch, download matching .exe
- Verify SHA256 (and minisign if available)
- Install to user-writable PATH (e.g., %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\dsr\bin)
- Support `-Yes`, `-Verbose`, `-Version`, `-Prefix`, `-CacheDir`, `-Offline`
- Respect non-interactive mode (`$env:CI=1` or `-Yes`)

## Logging
- Human-readable logs to stderr
- Optional JSON output for automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.ps1 parity with install.sh (flags, logs)
- [ ] Works on Windows 10/11 without admin
- [ ] Signature verification supported when minisign present
- [ ] Documented usage in README","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:27.874310491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:03.227926195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.359019185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.618811604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.857268243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7","title":"Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts","description":"# Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts

## Purpose
Define the non-negotiable contracts that every `dsr` command adheres to: CLI flags, output streams, JSON schema, exit codes, config layering, and runtime guardrails.

## Why this matters
Reliability depends on consistency. If command behavior varies (output format, exit codes, config resolution, or path handling), automation becomes brittle and debugging becomes guesswork. This theme establishes the foundation all other themes build on.

## Scope (Must-Haves)
- **Global CLI contract** and flag behavior (incl. `--json`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run`, `--verbose`, `--quiet`)
- **Stream separation**: stderr for humans, stdout for JSON/paths only
- **Exit codes** aligned with CLI contract
- **XDG layout** for config/state/cache directories
  - `~/.config/dsr/config.yaml`
  - `~/.config/dsr/repos.d/*.yaml`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/run.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/builds/*.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/latest -> YYYY-MM-DD`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/`
- **Schema compliance** for all JSON outputs
- **Runtime guardrails** (Bash version check, path normalization, safe file ops)

## Best-Practice Parity from ru (repo_updater)
- **Bash 4+ check** with macOS Homebrew guidance when interactive
- **Path normalization** helper (accept absolute or `~`, reject relative)
- **No parsing of human output**: always use structured JSON (gh api, git plumbing)
- **Deterministic dry-run** output (stable ordering)
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** respected globally
- **Run ID + log correlation** for every command execution

## Implementation Notes
- Provide a small core helper module (e.g., `src/runtime.sh`) with:
  - `require_bash_4` (Bash version gating + instructions)
  - `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default`
  - `safe_path_under` / `safe_delete` (allowlist-based safeguards)
  - `run_cmd` (captures stdout/stderr + exit code without `set -e`)
- Main entrypoint must call `require_bash_4` early and export `DSR_NON_INTERACTIVE` / `DSR_NO_COLOR` from flags.
- Logging should write to **date-based log dirs** and update `logs/latest` safely (atomic symlink update).
- **No global cd**: all git operations use `git -C <repo>`

## Non-goals
- Implementing build/release logic (covered by other themes)
- Host-specific tooling setup (Theme 2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every command follows stream separation and exit codes
- [ ] Bash 4+ enforced with clear guidance on macOS/Linux
- [ ] Config/state/cache paths are normalized and absolute
- [ ] Safe delete/cleanup helpers prevent non-dsr path removal
- [ ] JSON schema compliance is testable for every command
- [ ] XDG state/log layout matches the spec (daily dirs + latest symlink)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:38.667058888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.1","title":"Define dsr CLI contract, exit codes, and JSON schema","description":"# Define dsr CLI Contract, Exit Codes, and JSON Schema

## Goal
Create a single, authoritative contract that every `dsr` subcommand follows. This prevents automation breakage and makes logs/JSON outputs predictable.

## Global Flags (apply to all commands)
- `--json`: machine-readable output only on stdout
- `--non-interactive` / `--yes`: disable prompts
- `--dry-run`: show planned actions without mutating state
- `--verbose` / `--quiet` / `--log-level` (debug|info|warn|error)
- `--config` / `--state-dir` / `--cache-dir`
- `--no-color`

## Runtime Rules (non-negotiable)
- Use `set -uo pipefail` (never `set -e`)
- No global `cd` for git ops; always use `git -C <path>`
- Explicit error handling: capture exit codes per step
- stderr = human output, stdout = JSON/paths only

## Exit Code Contract
- `0` success
- `1` partial failure (some targets/repos failed)
- `2` conflicts (locks, in-progress run, concurrent watch)
- `3` dependency/system error (missing gh auth, docker, etc.)
- `4` invalid arguments/config
- `5` interrupted/aborted

## JSON Output Contract (stdout only)
Top-level required fields:
- `command`, `status`, `exit_code`
- `run_id`, `started_at`, `duration_ms`
- `tool` / `version` (when applicable)
- `artifacts[]` (name, target, sha256, size_bytes)
- `warnings[]` / `errors[]` (code + message)
- `details` (command-specific payload)

## Schema Versioning
- Include `schema_version` in JSON output
- Schema changes are additive only

## Success Criteria
- [ ] CLI contract documented (README + `dsr help`)
- [ ] JSON schema defined per command
- [ ] Exit codes tested for all commands
- [ ] Examples show stderr/stdout separation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:29.014069854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:07:05.750189965Z","close_reason":"CLI contract documented in docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md, JSON schemas created in schemas/, test fixtures and validation script created","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.2","title":"Implement dsr doctor: dependency + auth preflight checks","description":"# Implement `dsr doctor`: Dependency + Auth Preflight Checks

## Purpose
Provide a single command that verifies system readiness and catches failures before long-running builds/releases start.

## Checks (minimum set)
- `gh auth status` (interactive + `--hostname`) and token scopes
- Git version + repo access (`git -C <repo> status`)
- `jq`, `curl`, `ssh`, `docker`, `act` availability
- Toolchain availability: Rust/Go/Bun (optional per-tool)
- Disk space on local machine + build hosts
- Network access to `api.github.com` and `github.com`
- `minisign`, `syft`, `cosign` presence (if enabled)

## Output Contract
- Human summary to **stderr**
- JSON to **stdout** when `--json`
- Exit codes follow global contract (dependency/system error = 3)

## Modes
- `dsr doctor` (full)
- `dsr doctor --quick` (only core deps + auth)
- `dsr doctor --fix` (optional: **non-destructive** helper suggestions only)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects missing dependencies with clear messages
- [ ] Detects gh auth or permission failures
- [ ] Produces JSON output for automation
- [ ] Safe in non-interactive mode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:44.481842827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:57.883603602Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:57.883579576Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive doctor command with all checks per spec: git, gh auth, jq, curl, yq, docker, act, ssh, build hosts (mmini/wlap), minisign, syft, network connectivity, disk space, config validation. Added --quick mode for core deps only and --fix mode for remediation suggestions. JSON output conforms to envelope schema.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:44.481842827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:57.837508854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.7.3","title":"Implement dsr status/report command (system + last run)","description":"# Implement `dsr status` / `dsr report`

## Purpose
Provide a single, fast summary of system health and the most recent fallback activity.

## Output (Human)
- Last run ID + timestamp
- Last build/release status per tool
- Host health summary (online/offline, disk)
- Queue/throttling status snapshot

## Output (JSON)
- `last_run`: id, timestamp, status
- `hosts`: status + metrics
- `repos`: recent throttle status
- `errors` / `warnings`

## Dependencies
- Config loader
- Host health checks
- Log/state reader
- CLI contract + status schema (bd-8cg)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fast (<1s) summary from local state
- [ ] JSON output matches schema for automation
- [ ] Does not trigger network calls unless `--refresh`
- [ ] Includes last run_id + manifest path when available","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:26:55.877550964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:54.917617462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:54.917580021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:34.790498931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:35.306317590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:35.831485663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:26:55.877550964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:01.305226431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:45.885074711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.4","title":"Secrets & credential loading (gh/PAT/slack)","description":"# Secrets & Credential Loading (gh/PAT/Slack)

## Purpose
Normalize how `dsr` discovers and validates credentials without leaking secrets.

## Sources (precedence)
1. Environment variables (DSR_GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK)
2. Config file entries (optional, discouraged unless encrypted)
3. `gh auth token` fallback

## Requirements
- Never echo secrets to logs
- Redact tokens in any JSON output
- Validate scopes (contents:write for releases; workflow for dispatch)
- Support non-interactive errors with actionable messages
- Never write secrets to disk unless explicitly configured

## Redaction Rules
- Any value matching token patterns must be masked in logs
- `config show --json` must redact secrets ("***")

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tokens resolved deterministically with clear precedence
- [ ] Missing/insufficient scopes detected early (doctor + runtime)
- [ ] Secrets never written to disk in plain logs
- [ ] Redaction applied consistently in structured logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:16.111579035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.109028754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:43.753410065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.903498843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.5","title":"Runtime guardrails: Bash version + path normalization","description":"# Runtime Guardrails: Bash Version + Path Normalization

## Purpose
Harden dsr runtime behavior to prevent subtle failures (Bash version mismatch, relative path writes, unsafe deletions) and mirror the reliability of `ru`.

## Requirements
- **Bash 4+ gate** with actionable guidance (macOS: brew install bash)
- **Absolute path enforcement** for `--config`, `--state-dir`, `--cache-dir` (accept `~`)
- **Safe temp dirs**: always use `/tmp` or `mktemp -d` (never create in `/data/projects`)
- **Safe delete helper**: allowlist deletions to `DSR_STATE_DIR` + `DSR_CACHE_DIR`
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** disables ANSI across all commands
- **Non-interactive** errors: never prompt; return actionable error messages

## Implementation Notes
- Add `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default` helper (ru parity)
- Add `require_bash_4` helper (exit 3 on failure)
- Add `safe_path_under <root> <path>` and `safe_rm <path>`
- Use helpers in prune/build/install flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bash 4+ required with clear macOS guidance
- [ ] Relative paths rejected (with helpful error)
- [ ] Safe delete helper prevents accidental removal outside dsr dirs
- [ ] Temp dirs created only under `/tmp`
- [ ] All commands honor NO_COLOR and --no-color","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:53.109459246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:20.832616929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:20.832599226Z","close_reason":"Implemented guardrails.sh with all required functions. 22 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:36:53.109459246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kr","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management

Centralize dsr configuration. Config at ~/.config/dsr/ including config.yaml, repos.yaml, hosts.yaml. Commands: dsr config show/edit/validate/init. Environment overrides via DSR_* vars.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Config loaded at startup
- [ ] Environment overrides work
- [ ] Validation catches errors","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:22:57.697466261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737659325Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737652281Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
